title: NYSE Bonds
summary: This source is an informational/product page describing the operational mechanics of the NYSE Bond marketplace, covering order types, trading hours, fee structure, auction processes, and post-trade clearance and settlement via DTCC/NSCC. It is not a research document, news article, or event-driven content.
key_points: NYSE Bond marketplace operates with firm orders matched on strict price/time priority; no subject orders are permitted
Trading hours: Early Trading 4:00 a.m. ET – 8:00 a.m. ET; Core Trading 8:00 a.m. ET – 5:00 p.m. ET; Late Trading 5:00 p.m. ET – 8:00 p.m. ET
Two daily bond auctions: Opening Bond Auction at 4:00 a.m. ET and Core Bond Auction at 8:00 a.m. ET
Execution fees are $0.00 per bond for all order sizes; liquidity provider rebate is $0.05 per bond with $50 maximum per execution
All DTCC/NSCC RIO eligible bond trades are submitted to NSCC as locked-in transactions without an omnibus account
Bonds trade in decimal increments to 2 decimal places and are represented by nine-character CUSIPs
why_it_matters: This source provides baseline operational documentation of NYSE's fixed income trading infrastructure. It is relevant for understanding market structure elements such as order matching rules, auction mechanisms, and settlement processes, but contains no analytical content, forecasts, or macro-relevant commentary.
market_structure_context: The page describes NYSE Bond's price/time priority order matching model, reserve interest handling (displayed orders yield to undisplayed at same price), two daily bond auctions (opening at 4am ET, core at 8am ET), decimal pricing to 2 places, and post-trade locked-in transaction processing via DTCC/NSCC with RIO interface. Settlement is T+2 for CNS-eligible bonds with accrued interest included in reported prices.
